statements.sql: select max(`version_number`) as aggregate from `ai_scenario_assessment_versions` where `ai_scenario_config_id` = 2

insert into `ai_scenario_assessment_versions` (`ai_scenario_config_id`, `version_number`, `status`, `disaster_type`, `difficulty`, `estimated_time_minutes`, `scenario_title`, `title_en`, `title_fil`, `generated_scenario`, `description_en`, `description_fil`, `learning_objectives_en`, `learning_objectives_fil`, `generated_questions`, `generated_language`, `change_note`, `parent_version_id`, `created_by`, `updated_at`, `created_at`) values (2, 2, 'under_review', 'Flood', 'medium', 60, 'Barangay San Roque Flood Crisis', 'Barangay San Roque Flood Crisis', 'Krisis sa Baha sa Barangay San Roque', 'The Municipality of San Miguel, a mix of urbanized and agricultural areas, is bracing for the impact of Tropical Depression "Auring." For the past 12 hours, continuous moderate to heavy rainfall has been observed. At 2:00 PM, the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) receives reports that the San Miguel River, which traverses several low-lying barangays including San Roque, has reached its critical level and is steadily rising. Automated rain gauges indicate sustained rainfall in the upstream catchment areas.

Barangay San Roque, with approximately 5,000 residents, is particularly vulnerable due to its proximity to the river and several informal settlements along its banks. Initial reports from Barangay Captain Dela Cruz indicate localized flooding in Sitio Malaya, a low-lying area, with water levels already reaching knee-deep in some homes. Power outages have begun affecting certain sections of the barangay, and cellular signal is becoming intermittent. Concerns are rising among residents, and some are beginning to self-evacuate to higher ground.

As the LGU's MDRRMO Head, you are at the operations center. Your team has activated the Incident Command System (ICS), and key personnel are assembled. The Provincial DRRMO has been notified and is on standby for support. You need to make rapid assessments and crucial decisions to ensure the safety of Barangay San Roque's residents and mitigate further damage, considering the escalating situation and limited immediate resources.', 'The Municipality of San Miguel, a mix of urbanized and agricultural areas, is bracing for the impact of Tropical Depression "Auring." For the past 12 hours, continuous moderate to heavy rainfall has been observed. At 2:00 PM, the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) receives reports that the San Miguel River, which traverses several low-lying barangays including San Roque, has reached its critical level and is steadily rising. Automated rain gauges indicate sustained rainfall in the upstream catchment areas.

Barangay San Roque, with approximately 5,000 residents, is particularly vulnerable due to its proximity to the river and several informal settlements along its banks. Initial reports from Barangay Captain Dela Cruz indicate localized flooding in Sitio Malaya, a low-lying area, with water levels already reaching knee-deep in some homes. Power outages have begun affecting certain sections of the barangay, and cellular signal is becoming intermittent. Concerns are rising among residents, and some are beginning to self-evacuate to higher ground.

As the LGU's MDRRMO Head, you are at the operations center. Your team has activated the Incident Command System (ICS), and key personnel are assembled. The Provincial DRRMO has been notified and is on standby for support. You need to make rapid assessments and crucial decisions to ensure the safety of Barangay San Roque's residents and mitigate further damage, considering the escalating situation and limited immediate resources.', 'Ang Munisipalidad ng San Miguel, na pinagsamang urbanisado at agrikultural na mga lugar, ay naghahanda para sa pagtama ng Depresyong Tropikal "Auring." Sa loob ng nakaraang 12 oras, tuloy-tuloy na naobserbahan ang katamtaman hanggang malakas na pag-ulan. Bandang 2:00 PM, nakatanggap ng mga ulat ang Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) na ang Ilog San Miguel, na dumadaloy sa ilang mabababang barangay kabilang ang San Roque, ay umabot na sa kritikal na antas at patuloy na tumataas. Ipinapahiwatig ng mga awtomatikong panukat ng ulan ang tuloy-tuloy na pag-ulan sa mga lugar na pinagmumulan ng tubig sa itaas.

Ang Barangay San Roque, na may humigit-kumulang 5,000 residente, ay partikular na bulnerable dahil sa kalapitan nito sa ilog at ilang impormal na komunidad sa tabi ng mga pampang nito. Ipinapahiwatig ng mga paunang ulat mula kay Kapitan ng Barangay Dela Cruz ang lokal na pagbaha sa Sitio Malaya, isang mababang lugar, na ang antas ng tubig ay umaabot na sa tuhod sa ilang kabahayan. Nagsimula nang maapektuhan ng pagkawala ng kuryente ang ilang bahagi ng barangay, at nagiging putol-putol ang signal ng cellphone. Dumarami ang pag-aalala sa mga residente, at ang ilan ay nagsisimula nang lumikas nang kusa patungo sa mas mataas na lugar.

Bilang Pinuno ng MDRRMO ng LGU, ikaw ay nasa sentro ng operasyon. Na-aktibo na ng iyong koponan ang Incident Command System (ICS), at ang mga pangunahing tauhan ay nagtipon. Naabisuhan na ang Provincial DRRMO at nakahanda para sa suporta. Kailangan mong gumawa ng mabilis na pagtatasa at mahahalagang desisyon upang matiyak ang kaligtasan ng mga residente ng Barangay San Roque at mabawasan ang karagdagang pinsala, isinasaalang-alang ang lumalalang sitwasyon at limitado ang agarang mapagkukunan.', 'Effectively activate and manage the LGU's flood response protocols and Incident Command System during an escalating flood event.
Demonstrate clear and timely communication with affected communities and relevant higher authorities despite challenging conditions.
Make informed and resource-efficient decisions for evacuation, rescue, and relief operations under pressure.
Prioritize and implement safety measures for both responders and the affected population throughout the flood response.', 'Epektibong i-aktibo at pamahalaan ang mga protokol sa pagtugon sa baha ng LGU at ang Incident Command System sa panahon ng lumalalang kaganapan ng baha.
Maipakita ang malinaw at napapanahong komunikasyon sa mga apektadong komunidad at mga kinauukulang mas mataas na awtoridad sa kabila ng mahihirap na kondisyon.
Gumawa ng matatalinong desisyon at may paggamit ng limitado ngunit epektibong mapagkukunan para sa paglikas, pagliligtas, at operasyon ng tulong sa ilalim ng matinding presyon.
Unahin at ipatupad ang mga hakbang sa kaligtasan para sa mga tagatugon at sa apektadong populasyon sa buong pagtugon sa baha.', bindings: Krisis sa Baha sa Barangay San Roque

The Municipality of San Miguel, a mix of urbanized and agricultural areas, is bracing for the impact of Tropical Depression "Auring." For the past 12 hours, continuous moderate to heavy rainfall has been observed. At 2:00 PM, the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) receives reports that the San Miguel River, which traverses several low-lying barangays including San Roque, has reached its critical level and is steadily rising. Automated rain gauges indicate sustained rainfall in the upstream catchment areas.

Barangay San Roque, with approximately 5,000 residents, is particularly vulnerable due to its proximity to the river and several informal settlements along its banks. Initial reports from Barangay Captain Dela Cruz indicate localized flooding in Sitio Malaya, a low-lying area, with water levels already reaching knee-deep in some homes. Power outages have begun affecting certain sections of the barangay, and cellular signal is becoming intermittent. Concerns are rising among residents, and some are beginning to self-evacuate to higher ground.

As the LGU's MDRRMO Head, you are at the operations center. Your team has activated the Incident Command System (ICS), and key personnel are assembled. The Provincial DRRMO has been notified and is on standby for support. You need to make rapid assessments and crucial decisions to ensure the safety of Barangay San Roque's residents and mitigate further damage, considering the escalating situation and limited immediate resources.

Ang Munisipalidad ng San Miguel, na pinagsamang urbanisado at agrikultural na mga lugar, ay naghahanda para sa pagtama ng Depresyong Tropikal "Auring." Sa loob ng nakaraang 12 oras, tuloy-tuloy na naobserbahan ang katamtaman hanggang malakas na pag-ulan. Bandang 2:00 PM, nakatanggap ng mga ulat ang Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) na ang Ilog San Miguel, na dumadaloy sa ilang mabababang barangay kabilang ang San Roque, ay umabot na sa kritikal na antas at patuloy na tumataas. Ipinapahiwatig ng mga awtomatikong panukat ng ulan ang tuloy-tuloy na pag-ulan sa mga lugar na pinagmumulan ng tubig sa itaas.

Ang Barangay San Roque, na may humigit-kumulang 5,000 residente, ay partikular na bulnerable dahil sa kalapitan nito sa ilog at ilang impormal na komunidad sa tabi ng mga pampang nito. Ipinapahiwatig ng mga paunang ulat mula kay Kapitan ng Barangay Dela Cruz ang lokal na pagbaha sa Sitio Malaya, isang mababang lugar, na ang antas ng tubig ay umaabot na sa tuhod sa ilang kabahayan. Nagsimula nang maapektuhan ng pagkawala ng kuryente ang ilang bahagi ng barangay, at nagiging putol-putol ang signal ng cellphone. Dumarami ang pag-aalala sa mga residente, at ang ilan ay nagsisimula nang lumikas nang kusa patungo sa mas mataas na lugar.

Bilang Pinuno ng MDRRMO ng LGU, ikaw ay nasa sentro ng operasyon. Na-aktibo na ng iyong koponan ang Incident Command System (ICS), at ang mga pangunahing tauhan ay nagtipon. Naabisuhan na ang Provincial DRRMO at nakahanda para sa suporta. Kailangan mong gumawa ng mabilis na pagtatasa at mahahalagang desisyon upang matiyak ang kaligtasan ng mga residente ng Barangay San Roque at mabawasan ang karagdagang pinsala, isinasaalang-alang ang lumalalang sitwasyon at limitado ang agarang mapagkukunan.

Effectively activate and manage the LGU's flood response protocols and Incident Command System during an escalating flood event.
Demonstrate clear and timely communication with affected communities and relevant higher authorities despite challenging conditions.
Make informed and resource-efficient decisions for evacuation, rescue, and relief operations under pressure.
Prioritize and implement safety measures for both responders and the affected population throughout the flood response.

Epektibong i-aktibo at pamahalaan ang mga protokol sa pagtugon sa baha ng LGU at ang Incident Command System sa panahon ng lumalalang kaganapan ng baha.
Maipakita ang malinaw at napapanahong komunikasyon sa mga apektadong komunidad at mga kinauukulang mas mataas na awtoridad sa kabila ng mahihirap na kondisyon.
Gumawa ng matatalinong desisyon at may paggamit ng limitado ngunit epektibong mapagkukunan para sa paglikas, pagliligtas, at operasyon ng tulong sa ilalim ng matinding presyon.
Unahin at ipatupad ang mga hakbang sa kaligtasan para sa mga tagatugon at sa apektadong populasyon sa buong pagtugon sa baha.
